description: A sandwich made with lobster meat served in a split-top bun, usually dressed either with mayonnaise in the New England style or with warm butter in the Connecticut style.
core_ingredients: Split-top bun or hot dog-style roll
preparation_steps: Cook and chill or warm the lobster meat depending on the style.
Lightly dress the lobster with mayonnaise or melted butter.
Fill the bun with lobster and optional seasonings or garnish.

health_advice.benefits: Lobster provides lean protein and the dish can be filling without being very large.
health_advice.risks: Calories and fat rise quickly with extra butter, mayonnaise, and oversized buns.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use a modest amount of dressing and let the lobster remain the main component.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional lobster rolls contain shellfish and are served on a wheat bun. A gluten-free roll can make it gluten-free.

common_variations: Lobster roll with celery and herbs
